products.name: JBL Charge 5
products.verdict: The best all-around portable speaker — powerful sound, long battery, and it charges your phone.
products.description: The JBL Charge 5 remains the portable speaker to beat. It delivers rich, room-filling JBL Pro Sound with a dedicated tweeter and dual bass radiators, wrapped in an IP67 waterproof and dustproof body. The built-in power bank charges your devices via USB-A, and the 20-hour battery means all-day listening. PartyBoost lets you link multiple JBL speakers for bigger sound.
features: JBL Pro Sound with tweeter + dual bass radiators
pros: Powerful, balanced sound for its size
Built-in power bank is genuinely useful
cons: No Wi-Fi or smart assistant support

products.name: Bose SoundLink Flex (2nd Gen)
products.verdict: Bose's best portable speaker yet — stunning clarity and deep bass in a rugged, pocketable design.
products.description: The Bose SoundLink Flex 2nd Gen packs Bose's signature clear, detailed audio into a rugged IP67-rated body that fits in a cargo pocket. PositionIQ technology automatically optimizes sound whether the speaker is upright, hung, or lying flat. With 12 hours of battery life, a built-in microphone for calls, and a surprisingly deep low-end for its size, it's the premium compact choice.
pros: Best-in-class audio clarity for the size
cons: 12-hour battery is shorter than JBL Charge 5
Bass is good but not thumping

products.name: JBL Flip 6
products.verdict: The most popular portable speaker for good reason — punchy sound, full waterproofing, and easy portability.
products.description: The JBL Flip 6 is the sweet spot of portable speakers — big enough to fill a room, small enough to toss in a backpack. The 2-way driver system with a separate tweeter delivers crisp highs and punchy mids, while IP67 protection means pool parties and beach trips are no problem. 12 hours of battery life and JBL PartyBoost support make it a no-brainer at this price.
pros: Punchy, clear sound with real stereo separation
Truly portable — fits in a water bottle pocket
cons: Bass could be deeper for bass-heads

products.name: Sonos Roam 2
products.verdict: The smartest portable speaker — seamless Wi-Fi and Bluetooth with Sonos multi-room magic.
products.description: The Sonos Roam 2 is unique: it works as both a Bluetooth portable speaker and a Wi-Fi smart speaker that integrates with your Sonos home system. Take it to the park on Bluetooth, bring it home and it auto-switches to Wi-Fi for multi-room audio. Trueplay tuning adapts sound to your environment, and Alexa/Google Assistant voice control is built in. It's the smartest portable speaker money can buy.
cons: Expensive for its size and output
Sonos app required for full features

products.name: Ultimate Ears Boom 3
products.verdict: 360-degree sound in a bombproof design — the adventure-proof speaker that floats.
products.description: The UE Boom 3 delivers immersive 360-degree sound from a cylindrical design that sounds great no matter where you're sitting. It's IP67 rated, drop-tested from 5 feet, and it actually floats in water. The Magic Button on top lets you play, pause, and skip tracks without touching your phone. 15 hours of battery life and PartyUp pairing for 150+ speakers make it the ultimate adventure companion.
pros: 360 sound means no bad listening position
Floats in water — great for pools
cons: Bass is present but not chest-thumping
Cylindrical shape can roll on flat surfaces

products.name: Sony SRS-XB100
products.verdict: The best budget Bluetooth speaker — surprisingly full sound and 16-hour battery at an unbeatable price.
products.description: The Sony SRS-XB100 punches way above its $59 price tag. Its compact puck shape hides a full-range speaker with passive radiator that delivers clear vocals and surprisingly present bass. The IP67 rating, 16-hour battery, built-in mic for calls, and included strap make it the perfect everyday carry speaker. It proves you don't need to spend $150 for great portable audio.
pros: Compact enough to clip onto a bag
Built-in mic is a nice bonus
cons: Sound lacks volume for outdoor parties
Bass is present but not deep

products.name: Marshall Emberton II
products.verdict: The best-looking Bluetooth speaker — iconic rock-and-roll design with 30 hours of battery life.
products.description: The Marshall Emberton II looks like a miniature guitar amp and sounds like one too. Its True Stereophonic 360-degree sound fills rooms with warm, rich audio that has a distinctly musical character. The 30-hour battery life is simply outstanding — nearly double most competitors. IP67 waterproofing, a multi-directional control knob, and the iconic Marshall aesthetic make it a statement piece.
pros: Gorgeous design that looks premium anywhere
cons: Sound signature may be too warm for some

products.name: JBL Xtreme 4
products.verdict: The party machine — massive sound, 24-hour battery, and a shoulder strap for taking the bass anywhere.
products.description: The JBL Xtreme 4 is for when you need serious volume. Dual woofers and dual tweeters pump out 100W of JBL Pro Sound that can fill a backyard or drown out a beach. The 24-hour battery, IP67 waterproofing, built-in power bank, and included shoulder strap make it the ultimate portable party speaker. Auracast support lets you broadcast audio to unlimited compatible speakers simultaneously.

products.name: Bose SoundLink Max
products.verdict: Bose's biggest portable speaker — audiophile-grade clarity with deep, controlled bass.
products.description: The Bose SoundLink Max is Bose's flagship portable speaker, delivering the brand's signature clear, detailed sound at serious volume levels. Dual custom transducers and dual passive radiators produce deep, articulate bass that stays clean even at max volume. The 20-hour battery, IP67 rating, Bose SimpleSync, and Bluetooth 5.3 with Auracast make it the premium choice for discerning listeners who want the best sound quality in a portable package.
features: Dual custom transducers + dual passive radiators
pros: Best sound quality in the large portable category
20-hour battery in a big speaker is impressive
cons: Bose app needed for EQ customization

products.name: Anker Soundcore Motion 300
products.verdict: The best value mid-range speaker — hi-res audio certification and smart features at a budget price.
products.description: The Anker Soundcore Motion 300 delivers features you'd expect at twice the price. It's one of the few budget speakers with Hi-Res Audio Wireless certification (LDAC codec support), and its SmartTune technology auto-adjusts EQ based on speaker orientation. The 13-hour battery, IPX7 waterproofing, and customizable EQ via the Soundcore app make it a ridiculously good deal at $79.
pros: LDAC support at this price is unheard of
Excellent app with deep EQ controls
cons: IPX7 not IP67 — no dust rating
Build feels plasticky compared to JBL/Bose
